items.title: Parallel serotonergic pathways influencing spinal cord circuits
items.author: Qian-Yao Sun, Martha W. Bagnall
items.content: <article>In this issue of Neuron, Fenstermacher et al. reveal that subregions of the descending serotonin system innervate separate structures in the spinal cord and are differentially active during locomotion. The results suggest fine-grained neuromodulatory control of sensation and movement.</article>

items.title: Rescuing specific memories by rejuvenating engram cells
items.author: Louisa G. Zielke, Tomás J. Ryan
items.content: <article>Partial cellular reprogramming can modulate aging-associated decline across multiple tissues. However, whether targeting memory-encoding ensembles within specific brain regions is sufficient to restore cognitive function has remained unknown. In this issue of Neuron, Berdugo-Vega et al. show that engram rejuvenation rescues memory deficits and restores epigenetic-transcriptional features and intrinsic excitability.</article>

items.title: Segregating glial cells in the gut
items.author: Eve S. Rowland, Lincon A. Stamp, Marlene M. Hao
items.content: <article>Enteric glia are key components of the nervous system, contributing to many aspects of gastrointestinal function. In this issue of Neuron, Muppirala and colleagues reveal that anatomical niches dictate enteric glial transcriptional identity, identifying Tacr3 as a specific marker for intraganglionic glia.</article>

items.title: Repeat expansion RNA elicits toxicity through hybrid G-quadruplexes with promoter DNA
items.author: Lien Nguyen
items.content: <article>In this Neuron issue, Liu et al. show that the C9orf72 expanded G4C2 repeat RNA forms hybrid G-quadruplexes with CG-rich promoter regions, which impedes RNA polymerase II. This process causes global transcriptional dysregulation in C9orf72 amyotrophic lateral sclerosis patient derived cells.</article>

items.title: Regional specialization in prefrontal cortex manifests in the reliability of task progression codes
items.author: Jennifer A. Guidera, Daniel P. Gramling, Alison E. Comrie, Abhilasha Joshi, Shih-Yi Tseng, Eric L. Denovellis, Clay N. Smyth, Kyu Hyun Lee, Jenny Zhou, Paige Thompson, Jose Hernandez, Allison Yorita, Razi Haque, Christoph Kirst, Loren M. Frank
items.content: <article>Guidera et al. found that single units in rat dmPFC and OFC code reliably for progression through a spatial alternation task at action- (dmPFC) or outcome- (OFC) related task phases, consistent with the known functional specializations of these areas. Reliable representations of progression are well suited to support task-phase-specific computations.</article>

items.title: 20 lessons in team science: Learning from the experience of the International Brain Laboratory
items.author: International Brain Laboratory, Hannah M. Bayer, Daniel Birman, Gaelle Chapuis, Eric E.J. DeWitt, Laura Freitas-Silva, Christopher Langdon, Inês Laranjeira, Petrina Lau, Liam Paninski, Samuel Picard, Charline Tessereau, Anne E. Urai, Matthew R. Whiteway, Olivier Winter
items.content: <article>Leveraging large-scale neuroscience datasets requires new collaborative approaches. The International Brain Laboratory (IBL) is a distributed, open-science experiment facing these challenges. To succeed, IBL developed methods for shared decision-making, division of labor, authorship/credit assignment, career support, standardization, and robust data analysis. The International Brain Laboratory et al. share these lessons learned to benefit other collaborative scientific efforts.</article>
